title: Healey refuses to alter RMV policy that denies undercover license plates to ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey refuses to change RMV policy denying confidential license plates to ICE, prompting potential legal action.
The Trump administration claims the policy endangers ICE agents and violates constitutional law, citing the Supremacy Clause.
The RMV limits confidential plates to federal vehicles in active criminal investigations, excluding routine civil enforcement.

title: Body of Quincy kayaker pulled from Boston Harbor
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: 72-year-old Tri Trinh was found dead in Boston Harbor after setting out in a kayak from Quincy.
Trinh's body was recovered near Thompson Island and transported to Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital for examination.
Authorities report no signs of trauma or foul play; autopsy results are pending, says Suffolk County DA.

title: South Shore school employee pleads guilty to stealing food, equipment for ‘Snack Shack’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Van Cott, former Plymouth food services director, pleaded guilty to stealing school resources for his 'Snack Shack' business.
Van Cott ordered thousands in equipment and supplies using school and USDA funds, running the scheme since 2014.
He faces a potential maximum of 20 years in prison and $250,000 fines for theft and wire fraud charges.

title: New Allston hotel wins design award
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Atlas Hotel in Allston won the Lifestyle Hotel Public Space award at the 22nd Annual Hospitality Design Awards.
Located near Harvard's Enterprise Research Campus, the hotel features a living room lobby inspired by 'third place' concepts.
The hotel's design includes works by Boston-affiliated artists and amenities like a fitness center and a rooftop bar.

title: JetBlue is ending service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue will cease operations at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport on July 8, 2026, due to a "capacity crisis."
Manchester-Boston officials are disappointed after efforts to promote JetBlue failed against challenges, including rising jet fuel prices.
Customers on affected flights can opt for alternate JetBlue flights or receive full refunds according to the airline.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about warmer temperatures into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Temperatures in Boston will rise from the 50s on Friday to 70s and 80s over the weekend.
Meteorologists forecast Boston's hottest day next Tuesday with temperatures approaching 90 degrees, earlier than usual.
A mostly sunny weekend is expected after a cool and cloudy Friday, continuing warmer trends into next week.

title: France announces Boston-bound World Cup squad with sitcom-style video
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France's World Cup squad will stay at the Four Seasons Hotel Boston and train at Bentley University.
France will face Senegal, Iraq, and Norway in their 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage matches in the U.S.
Didier Deschamps trims the squad to 26, omitting stars like Florian Thauvin and Eduardo Camavinga.

title: Cape Cod judge faces domestic violence charges
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Judge Robert A. Welsh III is charged with assault and battery on a household member aged 60 or older.
Due to a jurisdictional conflict, the case is transferred to Brighton District Court for arraignment.
The affidavit notes the case's potential for publicity and the impoundment of sensitive case documents until arraignment.

title: 2026 summer movie preview: 26 must-watch films coming soon
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon stars as Odysseus in Christopher Nolan's adaptation of 'The Odyssey,' releasing July 17.
Two Massachusetts-filmed movies, 'Tony' and 'Super Troopers 3,' are set for August 2026 releases.
New 'Star Wars' movie among 26 must-watch films from May to September 2026.

title: ‘If you earn an A, you deserve the A’: Readers react to Harvard’s proposed grade cap
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Harvard is considering capping A grades at 20% per class, with a vote on the proposal this week.
Readers are divided; 58% oppose the cap, while 36% support it, citing grade inflation concerns.
Opponents argue that grades should reflect individual achievement, not a quota system that limits A's.
